patterns.name: 29th-Day Tactic
patterns.mentalModel: The invoice is due tomorrow. Why wait 29 days to raise a 'Missing POD' dispute? Let me check their history — they likely do this to freeze the dunning clock and steal another 14 days of terms.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join Open AR to Dispute Case via BELNR.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Map Invoice_Due_Date vs Dispute_Create_Date.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT DATEDIFF(day, UDM.CREATE_TIME, BSID.ZFBDT)
AS Proximity WHERE Proximity BETWEEN 0 AND 2

patterns.name: Dispute Code Hopping
patterns.mentalModel: I just sent them the proof resolving their 'Pricing Error' dispute yesterday, and today they opened a new 'Short Shipment' dispute on the exact same invoice? They are just throwing excuses at the wall to buy time.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Track unique Dispute IDs per Invoice BELNR.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Log frequency of REASON_CODE changes over time.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT REASON_CODE)
FROM UDM_DISPUTE GROUP BY BELNR
HAVING COUNT > 1

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF >= 5 Codes OR Pattern > 90 days

patterns.name: Dispute Splitting
patterns.mentalModel: Why did they submit ten separate $9,500 disputes instead of one $95,000 dispute? They must have figured out that our automated write-off or junior analyst approval threshold is $10,000.

oracle: HZ_CUST_ACCOUNTS — customer grouping for aggregate view

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Link multiple disputes to one parent Customer KUNNR.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Sum dispute creation events within a 24-hour window.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT SUM(AMT_DISPUTED) FROM UDM_DISPUTE
WHERE CREATE_TIME = TODAY
GROUP BY KUNNR

patterns.name: Friday Afternoon Slicing
patterns.mentalModel: It's 4 PM on the last Friday of the month. They just dumped a massive batch payment riddled with $40 micro-deductions. They know I am rushed for financial close and hoping I'll just write them off rather than reconcile 50 line items.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Link Bank Statement Items to Cleared Line Items.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Flag AUGDT (Clearing Date) falling on Month-End Friday.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT COUNT(BELNR) FROM BSAD
WHERE DAYOFWEEK(AUGDT)=6 AND WRBTR < 50
GROUP BY KUNNR

patterns.name: Price Tolerance Exploitation
patterns.mentalModel: This customer consistently short-pays by exactly $48 or $49. It's not a real pricing error; they have reverse-engineered our $50 standard tolerance limit and are skimming the difference on every order.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Map Reason Code (RSTGR) to short-pay amount.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track historical short-pay amounts upon clearing.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT (50 - WRBTR_SHORTPAY) AS Delta
WHERE Delta BETWEEN 0 AND 5
AND RSTGR = 'TOLERANCE'

patterns.name: Attachment Omission
patterns.mentalModel: They logged a dispute in the portal but attached zero backup documentation. They know it will take me 3 days to email them for the proof, and another week for them to reply, securing them 10 days of free float.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Link Dispute ID to Business Document Service.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Check for document attachment event at CREATE_TIME.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(BDS.DOC_ID)=0
THEN 'Null' ELSE 'Attached' END
FROM UDM_DISPUTE

patterns.name: Freight/Tax Skimming
patterns.mentalModel: The payment perfectly matches the goods value, but the freight and tax lines are left unpaid. They are hoping the cash app team just clears the product line and leaves the tax to rot in a dispute queue.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Map AR line items to SD condition types (Freight/Tax).
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Match partial payment specifically against Freight condition.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT * FROM BSEG JOIN PRCD_ELEMENTS
WHERE BSEG.WRBTR=(PRCD.NET_VALUE-PRCD.TAX)

patterns.name: Month-End RMA Dump
patterns.mentalModel: Why is this customer requesting 50 Return Material Authorizations on the 28th of the month? They aren't actually returning the goods; they just want the credits to hit so their month-end statement looks clean.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Identify Return Orders (AUART='RE') by Customer.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Count RMAs created in the last 3 days of the month.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT COUNT(VBELN) FROM VBAK
WHERE AUART='RE' AND DAY(ERDAT)>=28
GROUP BY KUNNR

patterns.name: 'Missing Backup' Loop
patterns.mentalModel: First they asked for the POD. I sent it. Then they asked for the original PO. I sent it. Now they want the signed contract. They are intentionally drip-feeding requests to string this out for weeks.

oracle: FND_ATTACHED_DOCUMENTS (DOCUMENT_TYPE) — tracks each doc request round

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Link CRM/Dispute notes to Invoice.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: NLP parse text for sequence: 'POD' then 'Contract'.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT COUNT(Text_Pattern) FROM STXL
WHERE Text LIKE '%POD%' OR Text LIKE '%PO%'
GROUP BY BELNR

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Request_Count >= 4 OR Loop > 30 Days

patterns.name: Missing PO Trap
patterns.mentalModel: They placed an urgent order without a PO number 30 days ago, and now they are rejecting the invoice because 'it lacks a PO'. They knew this would happen and are using their own internal compliance as a shield.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Map Sales Order PO field to Dispute Reason.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track if SO was created with Null PO, disputed 30 days later.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT 1 FROM VBAK JOIN UDM_DISPUTE
WHERE VBAK.BSTNK IS NULL
AND UDM.REASON = 'NO PO'

patterns.name: Approval Musical Chairs
patterns.mentalModel: I emailed AP, they told me to ask Procurement. Procurement told me to ask the Plant Manager. The Plant Manager told me to go back to AP. They are passing the buck to intentionally run out the collection timer.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Link Change Docs to Customer/Invoice owner.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Count changes to Contact_Person or Owner field.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT COUNT(CHANGENR) FROM CDPOS
WHERE FNAME='CONTACT'
GROUP BY OBJECTID HAVING > 2

patterns.name: System Migration Stall
patterns.mentalModel: They claim their 'new ERP is going live' and they can't process payments for 3 weeks. It might be true, but it's not my problem to fund their IT project. We need a hard Promise-to-Pay date.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Extract Collection notes linked to Customer KUNNR.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Parse text timeline for migration excuses.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT KUNNR FROM FDM_DCOLL
WHERE NOTE_TEXT LIKE '%ERP%'
OR NOTE_TEXT LIKE '%Migration%'

patterns.name: Legal Entity Shell Game
patterns.mentalModel: They ordered the goods under Subsidiary A, but rejected the invoice saying it should be billed to Subsidiary B. They do this to force a cancel-and-rebill, restarting the 30-day payment term from scratch.

oracle: HZ_CUST_ACCOUNTS (ACCOUNT_NUMBER, STATUS) — multiple accounts same parent

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Map Sold-To vs. Bill-To vs. Payer entities.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track disputes triggered specifically by Payer mismatch.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM UDM_DISPUTE
WHERE REASON='WRONG ENTITY'
GROUP BY PARENT_KUNNR
originalDAG: Update_CRM_Task(Alert Master Data Team to fix Entity routing)

actionChain.name: Legal Entity Shell Game — action chain
steps: Update_CRM_Task(Alert Master Data Team to fix Entity routing)

patterns.name: Portal Sabotage
patterns.mentalModel: They keep uploading our invoices to their Ariba portal using the wrong XML format, causing an auto-rejection. They then blame 'system errors' for the payment delay.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Map external portal status to SAP VBRK (Invoice).
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Count portal rejections and re-submissions.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT COUNT(Status) FROM Portal_Logs
WHERE Status='REJECTED'
GROUP BY Invoice_ID

patterns.name: Invoice Format Grievance
patterns.mentalModel: The invoice was sent 45 days ago. Why are they waiting until it is 15 days overdue to complain that our PDF doesn't have their preferred cost center code on it? This is a stall.

oracle: IEX_NOTES_TL — collector notes on format complaints [EBS]

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Map Invoice creation to format-specific dispute.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Calculate lag between delivery and format complaint.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT DATEDIFF(day, VBRK.ERDAT, UDM.CREATE_TIME)
WHERE UDM.REASON='FORMAT' AND Diff > 30

patterns.name: Disconnected AP Alias
patterns.mentalModel: Every time I email 'AP@company.com', I get an auto-reply or a bounce-back. They gave us a dead inbox on purpose so they can claim they 'never received the invoice'.

oracle: [Both Fusion and EBS via HZ schema]

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Map collector email logs to Customer KNA1.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track automated bounce-backs or generic out-of-office.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT COUNT(Bounce)/COUNT(Sent)
FROM Email_Logs
WHERE Customer_ID=KNA1.KUNNR

patterns.name: Check is in the Mail
patterns.mentalModel: They always promise a check is cut, but it takes 14 days to arrive. They are intentionally exploiting postal float time to hold onto their cash longer. I need to force them to ACH.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Map Promise-to-Pay to actual Clearing Date.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Measure variance between promised check date and clearing.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT AVG(DATEDIFF(day, P2P_DATE, AUGDT))
FROM FDM_P2P JOIN BSAD
WHERE METHOD='CHECK'

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Float 7–14 Days OR Repeat_Check_Promises

actionChain.name: Check is in the Mail — action chain

patterns.name: Bank Holiday Arbitrage
patterns.mentalModel: They blame a local bank holiday in their country for the wire delay, even though our contract mandates payment to our US entity. They use global holidays as a convenient excuse for 3-4 day delays.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Map Customer Country (KNA1-LAND1) to Calendar.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Cross-reference late payment dates with regional bank holidays.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT 1 FROM BSID JOIN SCAL
WHERE BSID.ZFBDT=SCAL.HOLIDAY_DATE
AND SCAL.LAND1=KNA1.LAND1
originalDAG: Update_SAP_Master_Data(Adjust Base Due Date to avoid regional holidays)

actionChain.name: Bank Holiday Arbitrage — action chain
steps: Update_SAP_Master_Data(Adjust Base Due Date to avoid regional holidays)

patterns.name: Bust-Out Fraud Precursor
patterns.mentalModel: They usually order $10k a month. Suddenly they placed 5 orders totaling $99k, right up to their $100k credit limit, and are dodging calls. They are about to go bankrupt or default, trying to grab as much product as possible.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Map Credit Limit to recent Sales Order volumes.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track order volume spike relative to historical monthly average.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT SUM(NETWR)/KNKK.KLIMK
FROM VBAK WHERE ERDAT>TODAY-7
GROUP BY KUNNR

branchingDAG.hitl: CFO approves legal hold — SLA: 2 hours

hitlGates: CFO approves legal hold — SLA: 2 hours

patterns.name: Partial Breadcrumbing
patterns.mentalModel: They owe $200k but wired exactly $5k. They aren't trying to clear the debt; they are throwing us crumbs just to reset the automated dunning system so their account doesn't go on credit hold.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Link Cleared amount vs. Original Open amount.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track payments that are exactly <5% of total due.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT (BSAD.WRBTR/Original_Amt) AS Ratio
WHERE Ratio < 0.05

patterns.name: Credit Block Bypass
patterns.mentalModel: Their credit limit is $50k. Instead of one $60k order (which would block), they placed six $9k orders across different days. They are circumventing our automated credit controls.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Map SO values to Credit Status.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Count multiple orders placed just under the block limit.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT COUNT(VBELN) FROM VBAK
WHERE NETWR BETWEEN (Limit*0.90) AND Limit
GROUP BY KUNNR

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Orders >= 5 OR Pattern_Duration > 30 Days

branchingDAG.hitl: Credit Risk VP approves limit reduction

hitlGates: Credit Risk VP approves limit reduction

patterns.name: Bureau Downgrade
patterns.mentalModel: D&B just downgraded their risk score last week, and instantly I'm seeing their payment velocity slow down by 8 days. Their liquidity is drying up fast; we need to cut credit exposure immediately.

oracle: AR_PAYMENT_SCHEDULES_ALL — DSO trend correlation [Fusion and EBS]

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Map external credit score to SAP Customer KNA1.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track Delta of D&B score vs Delta of DSO over 30 days.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT (Current_Score-Past_Score),
(Current_DSO-Past_DSO)
FROM Risk_Model

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Score_Drop > 30 Points OR Below_Threshold

patterns.name: Threshold Skimming
patterns.mentalModel: Our manual review limit is $10k. Their overdue balance keeps hitting $9,800, then they make a tiny payment, then order more. They are intentionally flying under the radar of manual credit reviews.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Aggregate Total Open AR per Customer.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Monitor total AR hovering just below the manual review limit.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT SUM(WRBTR) FROM BSID
GROUP BY KUNNR
HAVING SUM BETWEEN 9500 AND 9999

patterns.name: Remittance Decoupling
patterns.mentalModel: They wired $1M but didn't send the remittance PDF detailing which 40 invoices to pay. They do this knowing the cash sits 'unapplied' on our ledger, making it look like they paid while we spend weeks guessing what to clear.

oracle: IBY_DOCS_PAYABLE_ALL or custom remittance inbox log

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Map Unapplied Cash (FEBEP) to Remittance Inbox.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Calculate lag between wire receipt and remittance PDF receipt.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT DATEDIFF(day, Bank_Date, Email_Receipt_Date)
FROM Cash_App_Logs

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Lag 2–5 Days AND Amount < $500K
ELSE IF Lag > 5 Days OR Amount > $500K

patterns.name: Duplicate Payment Bait
patterns.mentalModel: They paid an invoice twice, immediately demanded a cash refund for the overpayment, and then two weeks later disputed the original invoice. They are trying to extract physical cash from us while leaving the AR open.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Link overpayments to refund requests and subsequent disputes.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track refund issue date vs new dispute creation date.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM BSAD JOIN UDM_DISPUTE
WHERE REASON='DUPLICATE'
GROUP BY KUNNR

patterns.name: Unapplied Cash Weaponization
patterns.mentalModel: They have $5k of old unapplied credit sitting on their account. They are using that as an excuse not to pay their $100k overdue balance, saying 'Apply the credit first' just to stall the larger payment.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Map On-Account cash to overdue invoices.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track customer requests to manually offset old credit vs paying.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT SUM(Unapplied)/SUM(Overdue)
FROM BSID GROUP BY KUNNR
originalDAG: Execute_SAP_Clearing(Auto-offset historical credit against oldest AR)

actionChain.name: Unapplied Cash Weaponization — action chain
steps: Execute_SAP_Clearing(Auto-offset historical credit against oldest AR)

patterns.name: Creeping Payment Terms
patterns.mentalModel: I am looking at their 12-month trend. They used to pay in 30 days, then 34, now 41. They haven't asked for a contract change; they are just silently stretching their terms to see if we notice.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Map Cleared invoices by Quarter.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Calculate moving average of actual days to pay across 4 quarters.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT QUARTER, AVG(DATEDIFF(day, AUGDT, ZFBDT))
FROM BSAD GROUP BY QUARTER

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Delta 10–20 days over 3 quarters
ELSE IF Delta > 20 days OR 4+ quarters

patterns.name: Unearned Discount Harvesting
patterns.mentalModel: They paid on Day 45, but they manually short-paid by 2%, claiming the early-pay cash discount meant for Day 10. They are just trying to harvest margin and dare us to charge them back for it.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Map Cash Discount Taken to Clearing Date.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Compare Clearing Date to the allowed Discount Date (ZBD1T).
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT KUNNR FROM BSAD
WHERE SKFBT > 0 AND AUGDT > ZBD1T

patterns.name: Strategic Shielding
patterns.mentalModel: Every time the system puts this client on credit hold, the Sales Executive logs in and manually overrides it, overriding our risk controls because they want their commission on the new order.

oracle: FND_USER (USER_NAME) — identify which Sales Rep overrode

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Map Sales Orders on Credit Hold to User IDs.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track how often Sales Reps manually release holds (CMGST).
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT USERNAME, COUNT(*)
FROM CDHDR WHERE TCODE='VKM1'
GROUP BY USERNAME
originalDAG: Update_CRM_Task(Alert Sales Director of override policy breach)

branchingDAG.hitl: CFO must approve all future credit releases

hitlGates: CFO must approve all future credit releases

actionChain.name: Strategic Shielding — action chain
steps: Update_CRM_Task(Alert Sales Director of override policy breach)

patterns.name: End-of-Quarter Ghosting
patterns.mentalModel: It's the last 2 weeks of the quarter. They know our Sales team is desperate to hit numbers and won't dare put them on credit hold, so they completely stop paying and ignore all my emails until Q1 begins.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Map Overdue items to Quarter-End dates.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track drop in communication/payments in the final 2 weeks of Q.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT COUNT(BELNR) FROM BSID
WHERE MONTH(ZFBDT) IN (3,6,9,12)
AND DAY(ZFBDT) > 15

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Duration 7–14 Days AND Amount > $100K

patterns.name: Chronic Promise Breaker
patterns.mentalModel: They went into the customer portal and logged a 'Promise to Pay' for next Friday. But looking at their history, they have broken their last 4 promises. I can't trust this date to pause dunning.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Map Promises to Pay to Clearing Status.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Calculate percentage of Promises marked 'Broken'.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN STATE='BROKEN'
THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*)
FROM FDM_P2P

patterns.name: Limit Increase Farming
patterns.mentalModel: A customer who asks for a limit bump every quarter, each time just after a clean payment, is farming my confidence - not funding real growth. Let me pull their KNKK limit-change history against actual order volume; if requests keep outpacing shipped demand, they are building headroom to draw down later, not to trade.

sap: KNKK (KLIMK, CTLPC) - credit limit and change log

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join KNKK limit-change records to VBAK order totals per customer over trailing 12 months.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Sequence Limit_Increase_Events against Order_Volume_Delta to expose requests that lead demand rather than follow it.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT COUNT(limit_change) AS reqs, AVG(order_delta) AS demand
FROM KNKK JOIN VBAK USING(KUNNR)
WHERE reqs >= 3 AND demand <= 0 -> flag Farming

patterns.name: Score Drift Masking
patterns.mentalModel: The bureau score on file looks fine, but when did we last refresh it? A customer whose external rating is quietly sliding while our cached score stays green is a deterioration hiding in stale data. Let me compare the KNKK internal risk class refresh date against the live bureau feed and flag any account coasting on an outdated grade.

sap: UKM_BP (RISK_CLASS, LAST_ASSESS_DATE) - internal risk class and freshness
KNKK (DTLIS, CTLPC) - last internal review date

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join UKM_BP internal risk class to the latest external bureau score by business partner.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track Bureau_Score_Delta since last internal reassessment date to surface unrefreshed deterioration.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT bp, bureau_now - internal_score AS drift, DATEDIFF(day, LAST_ASSESS_DATE, GETDATE()) AS age
FROM UKM_BP
WHERE drift <= -50 AND age > 90 -> flag Masking

branchingDAG.condition: IF Drift Between -20 AND 0 AND Age <= 90

patterns.name: Pre-Ask Payment Polish
patterns.mentalModel: Suddenly this chronic late-payer is settling every invoice early - right before a large limit request lands. That is not reform, that is choreography. Let me pull the BSAD clearing history and check whether the good behavior is a short window engineered to dress up the risk profile just before a big ask.

sap: BSAD (AUGDT, ZFBDT) - cleared item pay date vs due date

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join BSAD cleared items to any open KNKK limit-increase request within a 60-day window.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Sequence Days_To_Pay over trailing months to detect an abrupt improvement clustered just before a limit ask.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT kunnr, AVG(pay_delay_recent) AS r, AVG(pay_delay_prior) AS p
FROM BSAD
WHERE r < 0 AND p > 15 AND limit_request_open = 1 -> flag Staging

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Improvement_Window < 90 Days AND Request_Open
branchingDAG.hitl: Credit Analyst approves deferral of limit request

hitlGates: Credit Analyst approves deferral of limit request

patterns.name: Bust-Out Setup
patterns.mentalModel: A brand-new account that pays small orders perfectly for two cycles, then abruptly triples its order size and stops responding - that is the classic bust-out arc. Let me fingerprint new accounts under 180 days: rapid limit ramp, thin master data, and an order spike are the tells that they are about to load up and vanish before the first big invoice comes due.

sap: KNKK (KLIMK, KLIMG) - limit granted vs used

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join KNB1 new-account records to VBAK order ramp and KNKK exposure for accounts under 180 days old.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track Order_Size_Acceleration and Utilization_Spike across the account's first payment cycles.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT kunnr, order_ratio, util
FROM KNB1 JOIN VBAK USING(KUNNR)
WHERE account_age < 180 AND order_ratio > 3 AND util > 0.9 -> flag BustOut

branchingDAG.hitl: Credit Committee approves account block for suspected fraud

hitlGates: Credit Committee approves account block for suspected fraud

patterns.name: Sales Override Pressure
patterns.mentalModel: Every quarter-end, credit-blocked orders get released faster than my team can review them - and it is always the same sales reps pushing. That is commercial pressure overriding risk discipline, not a data problem. Let me trace VBAK block-release events against my risk queue: when releases cluster at period close on high-exposure accounts, revenue is being booked on credit I never actually cleared.

sap: VBAK (CMGST, CMFRE) - credit status and release flag
VBFA (release event log) - who released the block

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join VBAK credit-release events to the releaser identity and exposure-over-limit at time of release.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Sequence Block_Release_Timing against period-close calendar and rep identity to expose pressure-driven overrides.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT released_by, COUNT(*) AS releases, SUM(over_limit_amt)
FROM VBAK JOIN VBFA USING(VBELN)
WHERE quarter_end_window = 1 AND over_limit_amt > 0 -> flag Override

branchingDAG.hitl: CFO office approves revocation of release authority

hitlGates: CFO office approves revocation of release authority

patterns.name: Hidden Parent Exposure
patterns.mentalModel: Five accounts each sit comfortably under their limit, but if they all roll up to one ultimate parent, my real exposure is the sum - and it could be far past prudent. Let me trace the KNKK hierarchy and UKM_BP relationships to collapse related payers into one group; concentration hidden across legal entities is how a single insolvency takes down a whole portfolio.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Traverse UKM_BP relationships to map all KNKK accounts sharing an ultimate parent party.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Aggregate Group_Level_Exposure across related entities and compare to any group-level cap.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT parent_id, SUM(exposure) AS group_exp, MAX(group_limit) AS cap
FROM UKM_BP JOIN UKM_ITEM USING(PARTNER)
GROUP BY parent_id
HAVING group_exp > cap -> flag Concentration

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Group_Exposure > Group_Cap AND No_Group_Limit_Set

Credit Committee approves group exposure freeze

patterns.name: Seasonal Peak Overreach
patterns.mentalModel: This account maxes out its limit every peak season, then coasts the rest of the year - predictable, but the peak is exactly when a demand shock would leave us most exposed. Let me pull the UKM_ITEM utilization curve by month and check whether the seasonal spike is backed by matching sell-through, or whether they are simply front-loading inventory they cannot move.

sap: KNKK (KLIMK) - limit against which peak is measured

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join UKM_ITEM monthly exposure to KNKK limit and VBAK order seasonality per customer.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track Utilization_Ratio month over month to detect predictable peak-season breaches of prudent headroom.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT kunnr, month, exposure/limit AS util
FROM UKM_ITEM JOIN KNKK USING(KUNNR)
WHERE util > 0.95 AND month IN peak_season -> flag Overreach

branchingDAG.hitl: Credit Analyst approves seasonal order hold
Credit Manager approves permanent limit reduction

hitlGates: Credit Analyst approves seasonal order hold
Credit Manager approves permanent limit reduction

patterns.name: Uninsured Exposure Creep
patterns.mentalModel: Our credit insurance covers each account up to a policy sublimit, but exposure drifts upward while the insured cap stays fixed - so the uninsured tail grows quietly. Let me reconcile UKM_ITEM live exposure against the insured amount per buyer; any account creeping above its policy limit is naked risk sitting on our books without cover.

sap: UKM_BP (insured amount attribute) - policy sublimit per buyer

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join UKM_ITEM exposure to the per-buyer insured sublimit stored on the business partner record.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track Uninsured_Gap as exposure minus insured cap over time to catch cover erosion.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT kunnr, exposure - insured_cap AS gap
FROM UKM_ITEM JOIN UKM_BP USING(PARTNER)
WHERE gap > 0 -> flag UninsuredCreep

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Gap > 0 AND Gap < Materiality
branchingDAG.hitl: Credit Analyst approves order cap at insured limit

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Gap > Materiality AND Insurer_Declines
branchingDAG.hitl: Credit Manager approves uninsured exposure hold

hitlGates: Credit Analyst approves order cap at insured limit
Credit Manager approves uninsured exposure hold

patterns.name: Collateral Erosion
patterns.mentalModel: A letter of credit or bank guarantee backing this exposure is only as good as its validity date and issuing bank - and both quietly decay. Let me scan the security records for guarantees nearing expiry, coverage ratios slipping below exposure, or issuers whose own rating has weakened; a lapsed LC turns a secured line into an unsecured one overnight.

sap: BSID (open items) - amount relying on security

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join UKM_BP collateral records to UKM_ITEM exposure to compute coverage ratio and days-to-expiry.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track Collateral_Coverage_Ratio and Validity_Runway over time to catch eroding security.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT partner, collateral_value/exposure AS cover, DATEDIFF(day, GETDATE(), valid_to) AS runway
FROM UKM_BP JOIN UKM_ITEM USING(PARTNER)
WHERE cover < 1 OR runway < 30 -> flag Erosion

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Coverage < 1 OR Runway < 30 Days
