patterns.name: Just-Under Tiering
patterns.mentalModel: Approval authority steps up at fixed limit tiers, and I keep seeing requests sized at 49,000 when the committee threshold is 50,000 - too neat to be coincidence. Someone is structuring asks to stay inside a lower approver's authority. Let me pull the distribution of limit requests against tier boundaries; a pile-up just below a threshold means the control is being gamed, not respected.

sap: KNKK (KLIMK, CTLPC) - requested limit and approval class
CDHDR/CDPOS (change docs) - who approved at which tier

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join KNKK limit requests to approval-tier boundaries and the approving user from change documents.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Analyze the distribution of Request_Amount relative to each Approval_Threshold to detect clustering just below a tier.
patterns.conceptualSQL:
SELECT kunnr, request_amt, tier_ceiling - request_amt AS gap
FROM KNKK
WHERE gap BETWEEN 0 AND 0.05 * tier_ceiling -> flag TierGaming

branchingDAG.hitl: Credit Manager approves rerouting to higher authority
Internal Audit approves suspension of approval authority

hitlGates: Credit Manager approves rerouting to higher authority
Internal Audit approves suspension of approval authority

patterns.name: Naked Remittance Payment
patterns.mentalModel: A payment lands in the bank statement with no invoice reference and no advice in AVIK. I check whether the payer has always sent references before and suddenly stopped. When a reliable remitter goes dark on references right as their balance ages, that is a deliberate friction move to buy float, not a clerical slip.

sap: FEBEP (KUKON, VWEZW) - EBS item, note-to-payee text
AVIK (AVSID, KOART) - payment advice header presence
BSID (BELNR, KUNNR) - open items to match against

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join FEBEP bank items to AVIK advice on payer bank key and flag items with null KUKON and no linked advice.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track the ratio of referenced-to-unreferenced payments per payer across the last twelve receipts.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT KUNNR, COUNT(*) FROM FEBEP LEFT JOIN AVIK USING(payer_bank) WHERE FEBEP.KUKON IS NULL AND AVIK.AVSID IS NULL GROUP BY KUNNR HAVING prior_ref_rate > 0.8 -> flag decoupling

branchingDAG.condition: IF amount matches a single open invoice within tolerance
ELSE IF payer prior_ref_rate > 0.8 AND balance_DPD > 30
branchingDAG.hitl: Cash App Lead approves on-account hold

hitlGates: Cash App Lead approves on-account hold

patterns.name: Duplicate Refund Bait
patterns.mentalModel: The same invoice number and amount arrives paid twice within days, and then a refund request follows. I fingerprint payer, amount, and reference across cleared and open items. If the second payment overpays an already-cleared invoice and a refund note is queued, this is duplicate-refund fraud fishing for cash out the door.

sap: BSAD (BELNR, WRBTR, AUGDT) - cleared items already paid
BSID (BELNR, WRBTR) - current open payment on account

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Compare incoming payment reference and amount against BSAD cleared items for the same customer within a rolling window.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Fingerprint invoice reference plus amount and count occurrences across paid and pending receipts over 30 days.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT BELNR, WRBTR, COUNT(*) FROM (BSID UNION BSAD) WHERE match_ref_amount GROUP BY BELNR, WRBTR HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 -> flag duplicate; if refund_pending -> escalate

branchingDAG.condition: IF second payment reference not found in BSAD cleared history
ELSE IF duplicate confirmed AND no refund requested
branchingDAG.hitl: Cash App Lead approves on-account parking

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF duplicate confirmed AND refund_request_pending
branchingDAG.hitl: Controller approves refund release after fraud review

hitlGates: Cash App Lead approves on-account parking
Controller approves refund release after fraud review

patterns.name: Short-Pay As Error
patterns.mentalModel: The payment is a few percent under the invoice and the memo blames a rounding or tax error. I compute the exact shortfall and compare it against the customer's history of tidy round-number deductions. When the gap lines up with a recurring deduction amount rather than real tax math, the error framing is cover for an unauthorized short-pay.

sap: BSID (WRBTR, WMWST) - open invoice net and tax

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Subtract FEBEP received amount from BSID open amount and classify the residual against known deduction reason codes.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track shortfall magnitude and roundness per customer across the last ten payments.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT KUNNR, (BSID.WRBTR - FEBEP.KWBTR) AS gap WHERE gap > 0 AND gap = ROUND(gap,0) AND gap NOT IN (tax_variance_band) -> flag disguised short-pay

branchingDAG.condition: IF shortfall within tax rounding tolerance
ELSE IF shortfall matches recurring deduction amount
branchingDAG.hitl: Deductions Analyst approves deduction case creation

hitlGates: Deductions Analyst approves deduction case creation

patterns.name: Unapplied Cash Weaponization
patterns.mentalModel: A payer keeps sending amounts that never cleanly clear anything, so their cash sits unapplied while their invoices age untouched. I aggregate unapplied balance versus open receivables per customer and watch the trend. When unapplied cash grows in lockstep with aging AR, the customer is deliberately making cash hard to apply to mask genuine delinquency.

sap: BSID (UMSKZ, WRBTR) - special GL unapplied posting
AVIK (RWBTR) - advice amounts that fail to tie out

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Aggregate BSID special-GL unapplied postings per customer and join to their open aged receivables.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track unapplied-balance trajectory against aged-AR trajectory over rolling 90 days.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT KUNNR, SUM(unapplied) AS ua, SUM(aged_open) AS ar WHERE UMSKZ = 'unapplied' GROUP BY KUNNR HAVING ua_trend > 0 AND ar_trend > 0 -> flag weaponization

branchingDAG.condition: IF unapplied clears within standard cycle
ELSE IF unapplied buildup correlates with rising aged AR
branchingDAG.hitl: Collections Lead approves account flag for review

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF unapplied balance exceeds credit exposure threshold

hitlGates: Collections Lead approves account flag for review

patterns.name: Lockbox Format Break
patterns.mentalModel: The lockbox file suddenly fails to parse for one payer because their invoice numbers shifted formatting or padding. I diff the current advice layout against their historical parse template. When a payer who always parsed clean starts sending a mangled format right as receivables age, the format break is engineered to force manual handling and delay.

sap: AVIK (AVSID, XBLNR) - advice header and reference format
FEBEP (KUKON) - EBS reference field parse target

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Parse AVIK/AVIP reference tokens and compare token structure against the payer's stored parse template.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track parse-success rate per payer and detect a step drop against their baseline.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT payer, parse_success_rate FROM AVIP WHERE format_signature != historical_signature AND prior_success_rate > 0.9 -> flag exploited parse break

branchingDAG.condition: IF new format still resolves to valid invoices
ELSE IF parse fails AND prior_success_rate high
branchingDAG.hitl: Cash App Lead approves parse template override

hitlGates: Cash App Lead approves parse template override

patterns.name: Multi-Invoice Bundle Defeat
patterns.mentalModel: One lump payment is meant to cover a dozen invoices but the total ties to no clean combination without a hidden deduction. I run a subset-sum against open items to find which invoices the bundle actually covers. When no exact subset matches and the residual equals a disputed line, the bundling is designed to bury a short-pay inside a big number.

sap: BSID (BELNR, WRBTR) - open invoices for subset match
AVIP (RWBTR) - advice lines listing intended invoices

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Run a bounded subset-sum of BSID open amounts against the single FEBEP receipt total for the customer.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track residual after best subset match across bundled payments per customer.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT best_subset, (receipt - subset_sum) AS residual FROM BSID WHERE customer = payer ORDER BY residual ASC LIMIT 1 -> if residual = disputed_amount flag

branchingDAG.condition: IF exact subset sums to receipt total
ELSE IF residual equals a disputed invoice amount

patterns.name: On-Account Dispute Shield
patterns.mentalModel: A payer instructs us to post their payment on-account instead of against the specific invoice they are disputing, keeping that invoice open and the dunning clock frozen. I trace the on-account request against any live dispute on the matching invoice. When on-account parking consistently shadows a disputed item, the parking is a shield to stall clearing.

sap: BSID (UMSKZ, BELNR) - on-account special GL item
UDM_DISPUTE (CASE_GUID, OBJ_KEY) - open dispute on invoice

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join BSID on-account items to UDM_DISPUTE open cases on the same customer and invoice reference.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track how often on-account postings coincide with an open dispute on the target invoice.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT KUNNR, BELNR FROM BSID JOIN UDM_DISPUTE ON invoice WHERE UMSKZ = 'on-account' AND dispute_status = 'open' -> flag shield behavior

branchingDAG.condition: IF no open dispute on the target invoice
ELSE IF on-account shadows an open dispute
branchingDAG.hitl: Collections Lead approves forced application over customer instruction

hitlGates: Collections Lead approves forced application over customer instruction

patterns.name: Cross-Entity Misdirection
patterns.mentalModel: The payment references our invoices but the funds landed in a sister legal entity's bank account, so it will not auto-clear in the right company code. I match the remittance references against open items across company codes. When a payer repeatedly routes to the wrong entity while their correct-entity balance ages, the misdirection is a stall dressed up as an honest banking mistake.

sap: FEBEP (BUKRS, HKONT) - bank statement company code and GL
BSID (BUKRS, BELNR) - open items in intended company code

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Match FEBEP references to BSID open items where the invoice company code differs from the receiving bank company code.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track frequency of cross-entity landing per payer versus their aging in the correct entity.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT payer, FEBEP.BUKRS AS got, BSID.BUKRS AS should WHERE ref_match AND got != should GROUP BY payer HAVING repeat_count > 1 -> flag misdirection

branchingDAG.condition: IF first-time mismatch and small amount
ELSE IF repeat misroute AND correct-entity balance aging
branchingDAG.hitl: Cash App Lead approves intercompany fund transfer

hitlGates: Cash App Lead approves intercompany fund transfer

patterns.name: Debit-Memo Netting Trap
patterns.mentalModel: The payment nets an amount the customer calls a debit memo, but no approved debit memo exists in our books. I reconcile the netted amount against posted credit and debit documents. When the netted value has no matching approved memo and recurs, the customer is unilaterally self-issuing offsets to shrink what they owe.

sap: BSID (BELNR, SHKZG, WRBTR) - open debit/credit items
AVIP (VWEZW) - advice citing debit memo number

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Reconcile the netted deduction cited in AVIP against approved debit-memo documents in BSID for that customer.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track unmatched netting amounts per customer over the last twelve payments.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT payer, net_amount FROM AVIP LEFT JOIN BSID ON memo_ref WHERE BSID.BELNR IS NULL -> flag unapproved netting; if recurring escalate

branchingDAG.condition: IF cited debit memo matches an approved document
ELSE IF no approved memo exists
ELSE IF recurring unapproved netting over threshold
branchingDAG.hitl: Credit Manager approves credit review for netting abuse

hitlGates: Credit Manager approves credit review for netting abuse

patterns.name: Format-Switch Automation Break
patterns.mentalModel: A payer rotates their remittance channel and layout between EDI, PDF, and free-text just often enough to keep our automation from learning a stable pattern. I monitor format-signature entropy per payer over time. When a payer's format churn spikes while everyone else stays stable, they are intentionally breaking straight-through processing to slow application.

sap: FEBEP (VGEXT, VWEZW) - external transaction type and note format

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Derive a format signature from FEBEP channel and note structure and compare successive payments per payer.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track format-signature change count and entropy per payer across rolling six payments.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT payer, COUNT(DISTINCT format_signature) AS variants FROM FEBEP GROUP BY payer HAVING variants >= 3 AND entropy_trend > 0 -> flag automation break

branchingDAG.condition: IF ML predictor resolves the switched format confidently
ELSE IF entropy high AND predictor confidence low
branchingDAG.hitl: Cash App Lead approves standard-format request to payer

hitlGates: Cash App Lead approves standard-format request to payer

patterns.name: Serial Frivolous Filer
patterns.mentalModel: This account raises a fresh dispute every billing cycle and almost none survive investigation. When I pull the case history and see a wall of rejected cases with the same customer, the pattern is not quality - it is a stall tactic to keep the dunning clock frozen. I decide fast rejection plus a behavioral flag beats re-investigating every claim.

sap: UDM_DISPUTE (CASE_GUID, STAT_ORDERNO, CREATE_TIME) - dispute cases and status
BSID (KUNNR, BELNR) - open items by customer
oracle: AR_DISPUTES (DISPUTE_ID, CUSTOMER_ID, STATUS, CREATION_DATE) [Fusion] - dispute records

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join UDM_DISPUTE to SCMG_T_CASE by CASE_GUID and aggregate rejected cases per KUNNR over trailing 12 months.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track dispute-open events per customer and compute the rolling rejection ratio over time.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT KUNNR, COUNT(*) AS Disputes, SUM(CASE WHEN STAT='REJECTED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)*1.0/COUNT(*) AS RejectRate FROM UDM_DISPUTE GROUP BY KUNNR HAVING Disputes>=6 AND RejectRate>0.7 -> flag serial filer

patterns.name: Reason-Code Hopping
patterns.mentalModel: The first claim was 'pricing error', we sent proof of the contract price, and now the same case suddenly reads 'damaged goods'. When change docs show the reason code mutating right after evidence lands, that is not a new fact - it is goalpost-shifting to keep the case open. I trace the CDPOS trail and treat the hop itself as the signal.

sap: CDHDR (OBJECTCLAS, OBJECTID, UDATE, USERNAME) - change doc headers
CDPOS (FNAME, VALUE_OLD, VALUE_NEW) - reason-code field changes

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join CDHDR to CDPOS on change number and filter FNAME on the reason-code field for each dispute CASE_GUID.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Sequence reason-code change events against evidence-submission timestamps to detect post-evidence flips.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT OBJECTID, COUNT(*) AS Hops FROM CDPOS WHERE FNAME='REASON_CODE' AND UDATE > Evidence_Date GROUP BY OBJECTID HAVING Hops >= 2 -> flag reason hopping

branchingDAG.condition: IF Hops = 1 AND before evidence submitted
ELSE IF Hops >= 2 AND flip occurs after evidence

patterns.name: Promo Pricing Gaming
patterns.mentalModel: They are disputing a pricing 'error' on an invoice that already carried a promotional discount. When I compare the invoiced price to the promo-adjusted contract price and they match, the claim is manufactured - they want the discount stacked twice. I pull the condition records and let the price comparison speak for itself.

sap: VBRK (VBELN, NETWR, KNUMV) - billing header and pricing procedure
VBRP (VBELN, POSNR, KBETR) - billing item conditions

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join VBRK to VBRP by VBELN and compare invoiced net value against the promo-adjusted contract price per line.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Compare the pricing-dispute rate on promo-flagged invoices versus standard invoices over the campaign window.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT VBELN, NETWR, Contract_Promo_Price, ABS(NETWR-Contract_Promo_Price) AS Delta FROM VBRK JOIN VBRP USING(VBELN) WHERE Promo_Flag=1 AND Delta < Tolerance -> claim unfounded

branchingDAG.condition: IF Delta > Tolerance AND promo not applied
ELSE IF Delta <= Tolerance AND promo already applied

patterns.name: Shortage vs POD
patterns.mentalModel: The customer claims a short shipment, but the signed proof of delivery on the delivery note confirms full quantity received. When the POD signature and delivered quantity contradict the shortage claim, the evidence is already in hand - I do not need a warehouse investigation. I match the disputed line to the LIPS quantity and the POD status and close it.

sap: LIKP (VBELN, PODAT, PODST) - delivery header and POD status
LIPS (VBELN, POSNR, LFIMG) - delivered quantity per line

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join LIKP to LIPS by VBELN and match the disputed line quantity against LFIMG where PODST confirms delivery.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track shortage-claim events against POD-confirmation timestamps to flag claims filed after signed delivery.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT VBELN, POSNR, LFIMG, Claimed_Qty FROM LIPS JOIN LIKP USING(VBELN) WHERE PODST='CONFIRMED' AND Claimed_Qty > 0 AND LFIMG >= Ordered_Qty -> contradiction

branchingDAG.condition: IF POD absent OR LFIMG < Ordered_Qty
ELSE IF POD confirmed AND LFIMG >= Ordered_Qty

patterns.name: Quality Claim Inflation
patterns.mentalModel: A cosmetic defect on a handful of units is being used to reject the entire shipment for full credit. When the reported defect quantity is tiny relative to the disputed value, the escalation is disproportionate. I score the claimed value against the defect ratio and cap the credit to the genuinely affected units.

sap: VBRP (VBELN, POSNR, FKIMG, NETWR) - billed quantity and value

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join UDM_DISPUTE to VBRP by billing reference and compute defect quantity as a ratio of billed quantity versus claimed value ratio.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track the ratio of claimed credit value to defect proportion across quality cases to detect inflation.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT CASE_GUID, DEFECT_QTY*1.0/FKIMG AS DefectRatio, DISPUTE_AMOUNT/NETWR AS ClaimRatio FROM UDM_DISPUTE JOIN VBRP ON ... WHERE ClaimRatio > DefectRatio*3 -> inflated

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF full-shipment reject AND DefectRatio < 0.05

patterns.name: Cross-Channel Duplicate
patterns.mentalModel: The same grievance just arrived through the portal, by email, and over the phone, spawning three cases against one invoice. When I fingerprint the invoice reference and amount across channels, the duplicates surface immediately. I keep the earliest case, merge the rest, and stop three analysts from working the same issue.

sap: UDM_DISPUTE (CASE_GUID, REF_DOC, DISPUTE_AMOUNT, CREATE_TIME, CHANNEL) - dispute intake
oracle: AR_DISPUTES (DISPUTE_ID, TRX_NUMBER, DISPUTE_AMOUNT, SOURCE_CHANNEL) [Fusion] - multi-channel intake

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Group UDM_DISPUTE by REF_DOC plus DISPUTE_AMOUNT and count distinct CHANNEL values within a short window.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Sequence dispute-intake events per invoice across channels to detect near-simultaneous duplicates.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT REF_DOC, DISPUTE_AMOUNT, COUNT(DISTINCT CHANNEL) AS Channels, COUNT(*) AS Cases FROM UDM_DISPUTE GROUP BY REF_DOC, DISPUTE_AMOUNT HAVING Cases > 1 -> duplicate cluster

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Channels >= 2 AND same REF_DOC AND same amount

patterns.name: SLA Deadline Gaming
patterns.mentalModel: Cases keep landing hours before the contractual resolution deadline, betting the auto-credit fires before anyone can investigate. When I see filings clustered against the SLA clock with thin evidence, that is engineered to exploit the auto-credit rule. I stop the auto-credit timer and force a manual review before any money moves.

sap: UDM_DISPUTE (CASE_GUID, CREATE_TIME, SLA_DEADLINE, AUTO_CREDIT_FLAG) - SLA timers
CDHDR (OBJECTID, UDATE, UTIME) - case change timing

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Compute the gap between UDM_DISPUTE CREATE_TIME and SLA_DEADLINE and flag cases filed inside the auto-credit trigger window.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track the distribution of case-creation times relative to SLA deadlines to detect deadline clustering.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT CASE_GUID, DATEDIFF(hour, CREATE_TIME, SLA_DEADLINE) AS HoursToSLA WHERE HoursToSLA < 24 AND AUTO_CREDIT_FLAG=1 AND Evidence_Score < 0.3 -> gaming

branchingDAG.condition: IF HoursToSLA >= 24 OR strong evidence attached
ELSE IF repeat deadline-gaming pattern across cases
branchingDAG.hitl: Collections VP approves auto-credit rule freeze

hitlGates: Collections VP approves auto-credit rule freeze

patterns.name: Dispute-Deduction Double-Dip
patterns.mentalModel: The customer opened a formal dispute on an invoice and separately short-paid it as a deduction for the exact same reason. When I link the dispute case to the residual deduction item and the amounts and reason match, they are trying to recover the same claim twice. I net the two together so we only ever credit it once.

sap: UDM_DISPUTE (CASE_GUID, REF_DOC, DISPUTE_AMOUNT, REASON_CODE) - dispute case
BSID (BELNR, RSTGR, WRBTR, KUNNR) - residual deduction items

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join UDM_DISPUTE to BSID residual items on REF_DOC and KUNNR where reason codes and amounts align.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Sequence dispute-creation and deduction-posting events on the same invoice to detect overlapping claims.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT D.CASE_GUID, B.BELNR FROM UDM_DISPUTE D JOIN BSID B ON D.REF_DOC=B.BELNR AND D.REASON_CODE=B.RSTGR WHERE ABS(D.DISPUTE_AMOUNT-B.WRBTR) < Tolerance -> double claim

branchingDAG.condition: IF dispute and deduction cover different reasons or amounts
ELSE IF same REF_DOC AND same reason AND matched amount

patterns.name: RMA Phantom Items
patterns.mentalModel: A return authorization is requesting credit for items this customer never actually bought. When I reconcile the claimed return lines against their invoice and delivery history and find no matching purchase, the RMA is fabricated. I refuse the credit and route it to controls because organised return fraud is a money-out exposure.

sap: UDM_DISPUTE (CASE_GUID, RMA_REF, MATNR, DISPUTE_QTY) - return-linked dispute
VBRP (VBELN, MATNR, FKIMG) - historical billed items

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Left-join RMA lines to VBRP and LIPS purchase history by MATNR and customer to find return items with no prior sale.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Compare return-request events against the customer purchase timeline to detect returns preceding any purchase.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT R.CASE_GUID, R.MATNR FROM UDM_DISPUTE R LEFT JOIN VBRP V ON R.MATNR=V.MATNR AND same_customer WHERE V.MATNR IS NULL -> phantom return

branchingDAG.condition: IF every return line matches a prior purchase
ELSE IF one or more lines have no matching sale
ELSE IF repeated phantom RMAs across cases

patterns.name: Legitimate Root Cause
patterns.mentalModel: Sometimes the customer is simply right - we invoiced the wrong contract price or delivered late against terms we actually agreed. When the evidence confirms our internal error, the honest move is to credit fast, resolve within SLA, and feed the root cause back so it does not recur. I classify it as an internal-process failure and close the loop rather than fighting a valid claim.

sap: VBRK (VBELN, NETWR, KNUMV) - billed price vs contract
LIKP (VBELN, LFDAT, PODAT) - planned vs actual delivery

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join VBRK and LIKP to contract terms and compare invoiced price and delivery date against agreed values for the disputed line.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Compare invoiced-price and delivery-date events against contractual baselines to confirm internal deviation.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT VBELN, NETWR, Contract_Price, LFDAT, Agreed_Date FROM VBRK JOIN LIKP USING(VBELN) WHERE NETWR <> Contract_Price OR LFDAT > Agreed_Date -> valid claim

branchingDAG.condition: IF invoiced price and delivery match terms
ELSE IF price mismatch OR late delivery confirmed

patterns.name: Promo Over-Claim
patterns.mentalModel: The customer deducted 12% off-invoice but the promo agreement authorized 8%. I pull the billing conditions against the negotiated allowance record and the delta is pure over-claim. If the excess is real and repeated, I deny the overage and open recovery rather than let it clear.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join deduction line in BSID to billing conditions in KONP via KNUMV to compare claimed vs agreed promo rate.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track Claimed_Allowance_Pct minus Agreed_Allowance_Pct per invoice over the promo period.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT BELNR, (Claimed_Pct - KONP.KBETR) AS Overclaim_Delta FROM BSID JOIN KONP -> WHERE Claimed_Pct > KONP.KBETR

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Overclaim_Delta > Tolerance_Pct AND Recurrence_Count > 2

patterns.name: Promo Stacking
patterns.mentalModel: One invoice, three separate promo deductions stacked on the same line. The programs were never meant to combine and the terms say non-cumulative. I match every claimed condition on the invoice against eligibility and strip the duplicates that violate the stacking rule.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Aggregate all promo condition types applied to one billing doc from KONV and test against non-cumulative flags in KONP.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Count distinct concurrent promo programs claimed per invoice line over time.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT BELNR, COUNT(DISTINCT KSCHL) AS Stack_Count FROM KONV GROUP BY BELNR -> HAVING Stack_Count > Allowed_Concurrent

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Stack_Count > Allowed_Concurrent AND Conflict_Value > Threshold
branchingDAG.hitl: Trade Marketing Lead approves program-stack ruling

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Stack_Count > Allowed_Concurrent AND Recurrence_Count > 3

hitlGates: Trade Marketing Lead approves program-stack ruling

patterns.name: Threshold Splitting
patterns.mentalModel: A cluster of small deductions from one customer all landing just under our auto-write-off limit within days of each other. That is not coincidence, it is a single large invalid claim chopped up to dodge investigation. I aggregate by customer and reason code, and if the combined value crosses the threshold I block the auto-clear and pull them into one case.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Group open deductions in BSID by KUNNR and reason within a rolling window and sum against the T043G auto-write-off tolerance.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Aggregate deduction amounts per customer per reason code over a 7-day sliding window versus the write-off ceiling.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT KUNNR, SUM(WRBTR) AS Window_Total FROM BSID WHERE WRBTR < WriteOff_Limit GROUP BY KUNNR, Window -> HAVING Window_Total > WriteOff_Limit

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Window_Total > WriteOff_Limit AND Cluster_Size > 3

patterns.name: Expired Promo Claim
patterns.mentalModel: They took a promo deduction dated this quarter but the referenced program ended four months ago. The condition record validity window is closed, so there is no live agreement backing the claim. I trace the promo code to its validity dates and deny anything claimed past expiry.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join the claimed promo condition to A004 validity dates and compare the deduction posting date against DATBI.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Measure Deduction_Date minus Promo_End_Date per claimed condition to flag post-expiry claims.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT BELNR, DATEDIFF(day, A004.DATBI, BSID.BUDAT) AS Expiry_Gap FROM BSID JOIN A004 -> WHERE BSID.BUDAT > A004.DATBI

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Expiry_Gap > Grace_Days AND Claim_Value > Threshold

patterns.name: Freight FOB Abuse
patterns.mentalModel: The customer deducted a freight allowance, but this shipment moved FOB-origin so freight was their responsibility from our dock. The Incoterm on the order does not support any freight claim. I check the delivery terms against the deduction reason and deny freight claims on origin-term shipments.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join the freight deduction in BSID to the billing header Incoterm in VBRK and test for FOB-origin terms.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Flag freight deductions where the shipment Incoterm is origin-based across the freight-claim stream.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT BELNR, VBRK.INCO1 FROM BSID JOIN VBRK ON KNUMV -> WHERE Reason = 'Freight' AND VBRK.INCO1 IN ('FOB','EXW')

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF FOB_Origin_Term AND Claim_Value > Threshold

branchingDAG.hitl: Deductions Manager approves freight recovery case

hitlGates: Deductions Manager approves freight recovery case

patterns.name: Slotting Fee Claim
patterns.mentalModel: A new-item-introduction slotting deduction hit us, but I cannot find that slotting fee anywhere in the signed trade agreement. Retailers try to slip slotting charges in as deductions when they were never negotiated. I match the claim to the agreement line items and reject any slotting fee that has no contractual basis.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Match the slotting deduction reason in BSID against agreed allowance conditions in KONP for the item's agreement.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Flag slotting-type deductions with no matching agreed offer over the new-item introduction window.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT BELNR, SGTXT FROM BSID LEFT JOIN KONP ON Agreement -> WHERE Reason = 'Slotting' AND KONP.KNUMH IS NULL

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF No_Agreement_Match AND Claim_Value > Threshold

patterns.name: Scan Allowance Gaming
patterns.mentalModel: The scan-based deduction claims far more units scanned than we shipped into that account for the promo period. Inflated scan counts are how customers pad a per-unit allowance. I reconcile claimed scan quantity against shipped quantity and cap the allowance at what the POS data can support.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join claimed scan quantity in the deduction to shipped quantity in VBRP for the same item and promo period.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track Claimed_Scan_Qty over Shipped_Qty ratio per account per promo cycle.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT BELNR, (Claimed_Scan_Qty / VBRP.FKIMG) AS Scan_Ratio FROM BSID JOIN VBRP -> WHERE Scan_Ratio > 1.0

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Scan_Ratio > Tolerance AND Excess_Value > Threshold

branchingDAG.hitl: Trade Marketing Lead approves POS data audit

hitlGates: Trade Marketing Lead approves POS data audit

patterns.name: Co-op Ad Over-Claim
patterns.mentalModel: The co-op advertising deduction exceeds the accrued co-op fund balance for this account. They are claiming more ad spend than the agreed accrual supports, and often without proof of performance. I compare the claim to the available co-op fund and require documentation before releasing anything above the accrual.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Compare the co-op deduction amount in BSID to the accrued co-op fund balance derived from KONP rate times qualifying sales.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track cumulative co-op claims versus accrued fund balance per account across the ad period.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT KUNNR, (SUM(Claim_Amt) - Accrued_Fund) AS Overdraw FROM BSID GROUP BY KUNNR -> WHERE Overdraw > 0

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Claim > Accrued_Fund AND Proof_Missing
branchingDAG.hitl: Trade Marketing Lead approves co-op overdraw review

hitlGates: Trade Marketing Lead approves co-op overdraw review

patterns.name: Price Protection Window
patterns.mentalModel: A retroactive price-protection deduction just came in, but the price change it references took effect well beyond the protection window in the contract. Price protection only covers inventory within a defined lookback, not months of retroactive rebilling. I trace the price-change date and the claim date and deny anything outside the agreed window.

sap: A004 (DATAB, KBETR) - price change effective date

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join the price-protection deduction in BSID to the price-change effective date in A004 and measure against the contracted protection window.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track Claim_Date minus Price_Change_Date against the allowed protection lookback per SKU.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT BELNR, DATEDIFF(day, A004.DATAB, BSID.BUDAT) AS Window_Days FROM BSID JOIN A004 -> WHERE Window_Days > Protection_Window

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Window_Days > Protection_Window AND Claim_Value > Threshold

patterns.name: Ageing Exploitation
patterns.mentalModel: A deduction is quietly ageing toward the auto-write-off cutoff with no supporting documentation ever provided. The customer is running out the clock, betting we let it lapse. I monitor the investigation ageing buckets and escalate anything approaching the write-off threshold before it clears by default.

sap: BSID (BUDAT, WRBTR, KUNNR) - open deduction age

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Compute deduction age from BUDAT in BSID and bucket open unresolved items approaching the auto-write-off cutoff.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track deduction age in days against the investigation-window threshold per open item.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT BELNR, DATEDIFF(day, BSID.BUDAT, TODAY) AS Age_Days FROM BSID WHERE STATUS = 'Open' -> HAVING Age_Days > (WriteOff_Window - Buffer)

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Age_Days >= Warning_Buffer AND Docs_Missing

patterns.name: Silent Delivery Bounce
patterns.mentalModel: The invoice posted clean but the customer swears they never got it - that is the collector's excuse three weeks from now. I check the output log against the delivery status: if the email bounced or the EDI 810 was rejected and nobody re-sent it, the dunning clock never really started. A silent bounce today is a 'no invoice received' dispute at day 45.

sap: NAST (VSTAT, ERDAT, KSCHL) - message output status
SOST (MSGV1, RECEIVER) - external comm send log

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join VBRK to NAST and SOST on VBELN to compare invoice post vs delivery confirmation status.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track Invoice_Post_Date against Delivery_Confirm_Date to flag outputs stuck in error with no resend event.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT n.VBELN, DATEDIFF(day, v.FKDAT, GETDATE()) AS DaysUndelivered FROM NAST n JOIN VBRK v ON n.OBJKY=v.VBELN WHERE n.VSTAT='2' AND NOT EXISTS (resend WHERE status='0') -> flag stalled

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Channel = EDI AND Reject_Code = Functional
ELSE IF Days_Undelivered > 10 AND Amount > Threshold

patterns.name: Exploitable Price Seed
patterns.mentalModel: When the wrong condition record fires, the invoice looks fine to us but the customer's AP team spots the delta against their PO instantly. I compare the KONV price on the billing doc to the contract condition and the order price - a positive delta the customer can prove becomes a short-pay they are entitled to. Seeding a wrong price today is a guaranteed deduction tomorrow.

sap: KONV (KSCHL, KBETR, KWERT) - pricing conditions applied
VBRP (VBELN, NETWR, MATNR) - billing item value

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join VBRP to KONV and back to sales order VBAP to compare billed condition value against contracted price.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Compute Billed_Price minus Contract_Price per line and trend variance frequency by customer.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT p.VBELN, (k.KBETR - c.CONTRACT_PRICE) AS Delta FROM VBRP p JOIN KONV k ON p.KNUMV=k.KNUMV JOIN Contract c ON p.MATNR=c.MATNR WHERE ABS(Delta) > Tol -> flag mispriced

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Delta Favors_Customer AND Amount < Threshold
ELSE IF Delta Favors_Company OR Amount >= Threshold
branchingDAG.hitl: Pricing Manager approves condition record fix

hitlGates: Pricing Manager approves condition record fix

patterns.name: Cross-Border Tax Miss
patterns.mentalModel: A cross-border shipment with the wrong tax code is a dispute waiting in the customer's compliance inbox. I check the ship-from/ship-to jurisdiction against the tax condition and the VAT registration - if reverse-charge should apply but we billed local VAT, or the withholding was missed, the customer parks the whole invoice until it is fixed. Getting tax wrong stops payment on the entire document, not just the tax line.

sap: VBRK (LAND1, STCEG) - country and VAT reg

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join VBRK to KONV tax conditions and J_1IEWT, resolving jurisdiction from ship-from and ship-to country keys.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Compare Applied_Tax_Rate to Expected_Jurisdiction_Rate across cross-border documents over time.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT v.VBELN, k.KBETR AS Applied, j.EXPECTED FROM VBRK v JOIN KONV k ON v.KNUMV=k.KNUMV JOIN JurisRate j ON v.LAND1=j.FROM AND v.STCEG=j.TO WHERE Applied <> EXPECTED -> flag tax error

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Withholding_Missing AND Vendor_Country = Treaty

branchingDAG.hitl: Tax Manager approves cross-border tax reissue

hitlGates: Tax Manager approves cross-border tax reissue

patterns.name: Reversal Term Reset
patterns.mentalModel: Why did someone reverse a clean invoice and reissue it verbatim? Nine times out of ten it restarts the payment-term clock and buries an aging invoice back at day zero. I trace the cancellation doc to its reissue and compare due dates and net values - same amount, new date, no credit reason is the tell of a term-reset in disguise.

sap: VBRK (VBELN, FKART='S1', SFAKN) - cancellation billing doc
BKPF (STBLG, STGRD) - reversal reference and reason

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Self-join VBRK cancellation docs to their reissued originals via SFAKN and compare payment-term due dates.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track Reversal_Then_Reissue events where net value is unchanged but due date moves forward.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT c.VBELN, o.NETWR, o.DueDate AS Old, r.DueDate AS New FROM VBRK c JOIN VBRK r ON c.SFAKN=r.VBELN WHERE ABS(o.NETWR-r.NETWR)<Tol AND r.DueDate>o.DueDate AND STGRD IS NULL -> flag term reset

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Same_Value AND Due_Date_Shift <= Terms AND First_Occurrence
ELSE IF Repeat_Reverser OR Due_Date_Shift > Terms

patterns.name: Stalled Pro-Forma
patterns.mentalModel: The goods shipped, the pro-forma printed, but the final F2 invoice never followed - so nothing is on the customer's books to pay. I watch the gap between delivery/pro-forma and the final billing doc; every day it sits is a day of revenue unrecognized and a day of DSO we can never earn back. A stuck pro-forma is a silent collection delay we inflicted on ourselves.

sap: VBRK (FKART='F5' pro-forma vs 'F2' final, FKDAT) - billing type and date

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join delivery LIKP to VBRK via VBFA to find shipped deliveries with a pro-forma but no matching final invoice.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track elapsed days between Goods_Issue_Date and Final_Invoice_Date per document.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT d.VBELN, DATEDIFF(day, l.WADAT_IST, GETDATE()) AS Lag FROM LIKP l JOIN VBFA f ON l.VBELN=f.VBELV WHERE EXISTS (proforma) AND NOT EXISTS (final F2) -> flag unconverted

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Lag > 5 Days AND No_Billing_Block
ELSE IF Lag > 10 Days OR Period_End_Approaching

patterns.name: Unaccrued Rebate Gap
patterns.mentalModel: We billed the customer at list, but their contract earns a volume rebate we forgot to accrue - so the balance sheet overstates receivables and the customer will claw it back as a deduction. I match the billed price against active rebate agreements; any invoice under a live agreement with no accrual condition is an unfunded liability. The gap surfaces later as a rebate short-pay we cannot dispute.

sap: KONV (KSCHL rebate accrual, KWERT) - accrual condition value

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join VBRP to active rebate agreements KONA and check for a corresponding accrual condition in KONV.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Compare Billed_Value against Expected_Accrual per agreement and trend the unaccrued gap.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT p.VBELN, a.KNUMA, (p.NETWR * a.RATE) AS ExpectedAccrual FROM VBRP p JOIN KONA a ON p.KUNNR=a.KUNNR WHERE NOT EXISTS (KONV accrual cond) -> flag unaccrued

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Gap >= Materiality AND Single_Agreement
branchingDAG.hitl: Revenue Accountant approves rebate accrual adjustment
Finance Controller approves rebate condition remediation

hitlGates: Finance Controller approves rebate condition remediation
Revenue Accountant approves rebate accrual adjustment

patterns.name: Unapproved Credit Memo
patterns.mentalModel: A credit memo is money leaving the receivable, so it must clear the approval matrix - but I keep seeing memos posted just under the threshold or with the workflow bypassed. I check each credit doc against the release strategy and the approver log; a memo above tolerance with no recorded approval is either error or leakage. Every unapproved credit is revenue we handed back without a signature.
patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join credit memo VBRK to the release-strategy and CDHDR approval log to verify a valid approver exists for the amount.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Detect credit memos whose value exceeds the approval tier but carry no matching approval event.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT c.VBELN, c.NETWR FROM VBRK c LEFT JOIN Approval a ON c.VBELN=a.OBJKEY WHERE c.FKART='G2' AND c.NETWR > Tier(a.LEVEL) AND a.APPROVER IS NULL -> flag unapproved

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Approval_Missing AND Amount In_Tier_1
ELSE IF Threshold_Just_Under_Pattern OR Amount In_Tier_2

patterns.name: Milestone Billing Gap
patterns.mentalModel: On milestone contracts, revenue can only be recognized as each stage bills - but I see completed milestones that never triggered their partial invoice. I reconcile the billing plan against confirmed milestone completions; an achieved milestone with no billing doc is a revenue-recognition gap and a customer who thinks they owe nothing yet. Under-billing a milestone quietly delays both revenue and the collection cycle.

sap: FPLT (FPLNR, FAKSP, FKDAT) - billing plan dates
VBAP (VBELN, PS_PSP_PNR) - order to WBS milestone

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join billing plan FPLT to milestone confirmations and check each due date for a released billing block and invoice.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track Milestone_Completion_Date versus Milestone_Billing_Date to expose unbilled achieved milestones.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT t.FPLNR, t.FKDAT FROM FPLT t JOIN Milestone m ON t.FPLNR=m.FPLNR WHERE m.STATUS='Complete' AND t.FAKSP<>'' AND NOT EXISTS (invoice) -> flag unbilled milestone

branchingDAG.hitl: Project Manager approves milestone billing release
Revenue Accountant approves unbilled revenue accrual

hitlGates: Project Manager approves milestone billing release
Revenue Accountant approves unbilled revenue accrual

patterns.name: Order-Change Duplicate
patterns.mentalModel: When an order is changed mid-flight, the billing run sometimes generates a second invoice for the same delivery - and the customer receives two docs for one shipment. I fingerprint invoices by delivery, material, and value; a near-identical pair from an order-change event is a duplicate the customer will refuse to pay twice. One duplicate creates a dispute that freezes both invoices until we prove which is real.

sap: VBRP (VGBEL, MATNR, FKIMG, NETWR) - billing item to delivery

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Self-join VBRP on delivery-material-quantity to find multiple billing lines pointing at the same delivery line.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Detect duplicate billing fingerprints emerging shortly after an order-change event on the same reference.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT a.VBELN, b.VBELN FROM VBRP a JOIN VBRP b ON a.VGBEL=b.VGBEL AND a.MATNR=b.MATNR AND a.NETWR=b.NETWR WHERE a.VBELN<>b.VBELN -> flag duplicate pair

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Both_Active AND Same_Value AND Post_Change

patterns.name: Intercompany Price Drift
patterns.mentalModel: Intercompany invoices must ride the agreed transfer-price markup, or the two entities book mismatched margins and the eliminations break at consolidation. I compare the IC billed price to the current transfer-pricing table for that material and corridor; any drift is a tax and consolidation exposure that surfaces months later in an audit adjustment. A quiet transfer-price error today is a restatement headache at year-end.

sap: VBRK (VKORG, KUNAG - IC partner, FKART='IV') - intercompany billing
KONV (KSCHL='PI01' transfer price, KBETR) - IC price condition
T001 (BUKRS, RCOMP) - company code to trading partner

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join IC billing VBRK to KONV transfer-price conditions and the transfer-pricing master for that material and entity corridor.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Compute Billed_IC_Markup versus Agreed_Transfer_Markup and trend drift by entity pair.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT v.VBELN, k.KBETR AS Billed, tp.AGREED FROM VBRK v JOIN KONV k ON v.KNUMV=k.KNUMV JOIN TransferPrice tp ON v.MATNR=tp.MATNR AND v.VKORG=tp.CORRIDOR WHERE ABS(Billed-AGREED) > Tol -> flag drift

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Drift >= Tolerance AND Corridor_Documented

branchingDAG.hitl: Tax Manager approves transfer-price policy review

hitlGates: Tax Manager approves transfer-price policy review

patterns.name: Post-Booking Edit Churn
patterns.mentalModel: This order booked clean, then got touched five times in three days. I check the change log - each edit nudges the requested delivery date or re-triggers the pricing snapshot. That is not fat-fingering, that is someone re-shopping the price and resetting the terms clock after the fact.

sap: VBAK (ERDAT, AEDAT) - create vs last change
KONV (KNUMV, KDATU) - pricing date on condition

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join VBAK to CDHDR/CDPOS on OBJECTID to count post-booking changes per order.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track change-event count and pricing-date resets over days since booking.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT VBELN, COUNT(*) AS Edits FROM CDPOS WHERE FNAME IN ('KDATU','VDATU') AND UDATE > ERDAT + 0 GROUP BY VBELN HAVING Edits >= 3 -> flag

branchingDAG.condition: IF Edits BETWEEN 1 AND 2 AND No_Price_Change

branchingDAG.hitl: Order Management Lead approves pricing-snapshot revert

hitlGates: Order Management Lead approves pricing-snapshot revert

patterns.name: Serial Credit-Hold Override
patterns.mentalModel: Every order this rep books lands on credit hold, and every single one gets released within the hour. When one rep overrides one hundred percent of their holds, the credit check has become theatre. I trace the release actor against exposure - the downstream is unrecoverable AR.

sap: KNKK (KLIMK, SKFOR) - credit limit and exposure

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join VBUK credit-status changes to release actor and KNKK exposure per rep.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track hold-to-release latency and override rate per user over rolling weeks.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT USERNAME, SUM(CASE WHEN CMGST='D' THEN 1 END)*1.0/COUNT(*) AS OverrideRate FROM CDPOS GROUP BY USERNAME HAVING OverrideRate > 0.8 -> escalate

branchingDAG.hitl: Collections VP approves rep release-right freeze

hitlGates: Collections VP approves rep release-right freeze

patterns.name: Threshold Split Gaming
patterns.mentalModel: One customer, one ship-to, one day - and three orders each sitting just under the approval ceiling. Add them up and it is a single deal that should have gone to a manager. Splitting the order to dodge the sign-off is the tell, and it starves the credit review that the aggregate value demanded.

sap: VBAK (KUNNR, ERDAT, NETWR) - customer, date, value

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Group VBAK by customer, ship-to and day; sum NETWR across near-simultaneous orders.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track clustered order values just below approval thresholds within a short window.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT KUNNR, ERDAT, COUNT(*) AS Orders, SUM(NETWR) AS TotalVal FROM VBAK GROUP BY KUNNR, ERDAT HAVING Orders >= 2 AND MAX(NETWR) < Approval_Limit AND TotalVal > Approval_Limit -> flag

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF TotalVal > Approval_Limit AND Same_ShipTo
branchingDAG.hitl: Order Management Lead approves aggregate order review

hitlGates: Order Management Lead approves aggregate order review

patterns.name: Phantom Rush Flagging
patterns.mentalModel: This order is flagged rush, yet the requested delivery date is three weeks out. Rush priority skips the standard ATP and pricing validation, so marking a routine order as expedited is a way to jump the queue without earning it. When the urgency flag and the delivery date disagree, the flag is the lie.

sap: VBAK (VSBED, VDATU) - shipping condition and requested date
VBAP (PRIO, EDATU) - line priority and item date

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Compare VBAK shipping condition and priority against requested delivery date gap.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track rush-flag incidence versus realized lead-time slack per user.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT VBELN, DATEDIFF(day, ERDAT, VDATU) AS LeadDays FROM VBAK WHERE VSBED IN (Rush_Codes) AND LeadDays > 7 -> flag

branchingDAG.hitl: Order Management Lead approves priority downgrade

hitlGates: Order Management Lead approves priority downgrade

patterns.name: Disproportionate Free Goods
patterns.mentalModel: Buy-one-get-one is fine until the free line is bigger than the paid line. I ratio the free-goods quantity against the billed value - when giveaways outweigh the sale, margin is walking out the door and it lands as a write-off downstream in billing. A padded free line is a discount in disguise.

sap: VBAP (ABGRU, PSTYV, NETWR) - item category and value

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Split VBAP into free versus billed lines and ratio free quantity to net order value.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track free-goods-to-value ratio per order and per sales user over time.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT VBELN, SUM(CASE WHEN PSTYV='TANN' THEN KWMENG END)*1.0/NULLIF(SUM(NETWR),0) AS FreeRatio FROM VBAP GROUP BY VBELN HAVING FreeRatio > 0.25 -> flag

patterns.name: Return Reorder Cycling
patterns.mentalModel: Same material returned, then reordered within a week - and the reorder somehow qualifies for a promo the original missed. Following the document flow, the return is not a defect, it is a reset button to re-price the same goods cheaper. The churn hits billing with credit memos and re-invoices for one unchanged unit.

sap: VBFA (VBELV, VBELN, VBTYP) - document flow return to reorder
VBAK (AUART) - order type RE vs OR

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Trace VBFA from return document to subsequent order for the same customer and material.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track return-then-reorder pairs and the pricing delta between cycles.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT R.KUNNR, R.MATNR, DATEDIFF(day, R.ERDAT, O.ERDAT) AS Gap FROM Returns R JOIN Orders O ON R.MATNR=O.MATNR AND O.ERDAT>R.ERDAT WHERE Gap <= 14 AND O.NETPR < R.NETPR -> flag

branchingDAG.hitl: Order Management Lead approves reorder pricing hold
Sales Manager approves promo eligibility block

hitlGates: Order Management Lead approves reorder pricing hold
Sales Manager approves promo eligibility block

patterns.name: Post-Approval Ship Swap
patterns.mentalModel: Credit cleared this order for the original ship-to, then the address changed after approval. Regional credit controls are keyed to territory, so swapping the destination post-approval slips the shipment past the control that would have caught the risky region. The credit check passed for an order that no longer exists.

sap: VBPA (ADRNR, PARVW) - ship-to partner and address

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Compare VBPA ship-to change timestamp against VBUK credit-approval timestamp.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track address changes occurring after credit release across control areas.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT VBELN FROM CDPOS C JOIN VBUK V ON C.OBJECTID=V.VBELN WHERE C.FNAME='ADRNR' AND C.UDATE > V.Credit_Release_Date AND New_KKBER <> Old_KKBER -> escalate

branchingDAG.hitl: Credit Manager approves re-check on new region

hitlGates: Credit Manager approves re-check on new region

patterns.name: Blanket PO Overdraw
patterns.mentalModel: The blanket agreement contracted for ten thousand units and the releases have already drawn eleven thousand. Each call-off looked small in isolation, but the running total blew past the ceiling. Over-releasing beyond contract means unpriced quantity is shipping, and billing will fight the customer over terms that were never agreed.

sap: VBAK (AUART) - order type contract vs release
VBAP (KWMENG, ZMENG) - release and target qty

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Sum VBAP release quantities per contract via VBFA and compare to VEDA target.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track cumulative release quantity against contracted ceiling over the agreement window.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT Contract, SUM(KWMENG) AS Released, MAX(Target_Qty) AS Ceiling FROM Releases GROUP BY Contract HAVING Released > Ceiling * 1.0 -> flag

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Released > Ceiling AND Within_Grace_Band
branchingDAG.hitl: Order Management Lead approves contract amendment
Sales Manager approves release block on overdrawn contract

hitlGates: Order Management Lead approves contract amendment
Sales Manager approves release block on overdrawn contract

patterns.name: Lead-Time Price Lock
patterns.mentalModel: This order carries a requested date months out on an item with a known long lead time. Booking early is legitimate, but the trick is locking today's price against a delivery far in the future to dodge a scheduled increase. When the pricing date and the delivery date drift far apart on a slow-moving item, someone is front-running the price list.

sap: KONV (KDATU, KSCHL) - pricing date on condition

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Compare KONV pricing date to VBAP requested date against MARC planned lead time.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track pricing-date-to-delivery gap relative to known lead time before price changes.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT VBELN, DATEDIFF(day, KDATU, EDATU) AS Gap, PLIFZ FROM VBAP JOIN KONV JOIN MARC WHERE Gap > PLIFZ + 60 AND Price_Increase_Scheduled -> flag

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Gap > LeadTime + 60 AND Increase_Pending

patterns.name: Manual Override Concentration
patterns.mentalModel: The system computes a price, yet a handful of users override it on nearly every order they touch. One override is judgement; a ninety percent override rate is a private price list. I rank manual condition entries by user - the concentration tells me where system pricing is being quietly bypassed and margin leaks before it ever reaches billing.

sap: KONV (KSCHL, KHERK) - condition origin manual vs auto

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Group KONV manual-origin conditions by order creator and ratio against total lines.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track manual-override rate per user across orders over rolling periods.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT ERNAM, SUM(CASE WHEN KHERK='C' THEN 1 END)*1.0/COUNT(*) AS OverrideRate FROM KONV JOIN VBAK GROUP BY ERNAM HAVING OverrideRate > 0.5 -> flag

patterns.name: Phantom DSO Compression
patterns.mentalModel: My DSO dropped four days this quarter, but I know we did not collect faster. When I overlay billing document dates against delivery confirmations and cash timing, I see invoices posted with backdated baseline dates and a surge of bill-and-hold entries with no goods issue. Someone is manufacturing DSO improvement in billing that never happened in collections or cash. I freeze the affected billing batch before it poisons the ageing and my board number.

sap: BKPF (BUDAT, BLDAT, CPUDT) - posting vs document vs entry date drift
BSID (ZFBDT, VBELN) - AR baseline date and delivery reference
VBRK/LIKP (FKDAT, WADAT_IST) - billing date vs actual goods issue
oracle: RA_CUSTOMER_TRX_ALL (TRX_DATE, CREATION_DATE) [Fusion] - invoice vs entry date

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join BKPF billing docs to BSID open AR and to delivery goods-issue records by VBELN to compare posted baseline date against actual delivery date.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track the rolling gap between AR baseline date and goods-issue date per billing batch against the reported DSO trend line.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT b.VBELN, DATEDIFF(day, l.WADAT_IST, k.ZFBDT) AS BaselineDrift FROM BSID k JOIN BKPF b ON k.BELNR=b.BELNR JOIN LIKP l ON b.VBELN=l.VBELN WHERE BaselineDrift < -5 -> flag AS backdated_billing

branchingDAG.condition: IF Drift BETWEEN -3 AND 0 AND Volume_Pct < 2
ELSE IF Drift < -5 AND BillAndHold_Flag = TRUE

branchingDAG.hitl: CFO approves DSO restatement to the board

hitlGates: CFO approves DSO restatement to the board

patterns.name: Provision Smoothing Gambit
patterns.mentalModel: Our bad-debt ratio is suspiciously stable while the underlying ageing is deteriorating. When I cross the write-off postings against subsequent cash receipts and collection notes, I find balances written off as uncollectible that were paid weeks later, or that still had active promises-to-pay. That is P&L smoothing dressed up as prudence, and it hides recoverable working capital. I restate the provision and pull the recoveries back onto the books.

sap: BSEG (HKONT, DMBTR, UMSKZ) - write-off line and special GL
BSAD (AUGDT, AUGBL) - clearing and later reversal

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join write-off postings on the bad-debt GL account to subsequent cash receipts and open promise-to-pay notes for the same customer.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track write-off events followed within 90 days by cash receipt or active collection promise on the same account.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT w.KUNNR, w.DMBTR FROM BSEG w JOIN AR_CASH_RECEIPTS_ALL c ON w.KUNNR=c.CUSTOMER_ID WHERE w.HKONT IN (bad_debt_accts) AND c.RECEIPT_DATE > w.BUDAT AND DATEDIFF(day,w.BUDAT,c.RECEIPT_DATE) < 90 -> flag AS premature_writeoff

patterns.name: Fragmented Exposure Blindspot
patterns.mentalModel: Credit says this buyer is well within limit, but I do not trust it. When I fuzzy-match customer master across account numbers by tax ID, address and payer hierarchy, the same economic entity is split across five accounts, each under its own limit while the aggregate blows past our single-name cap. Collections chases them as strangers and cash application cannot net them. I consolidate the exposure before one default takes us by surprise.

sap: KNA1 (STCD1, STCEG, NAME1, ORT01) - tax number, VAT, name, city for matching
KNKK (KLIMK, KKBER) - credit limit per control area

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Self-join customer master on normalized tax ID and address to collapse multiple KUNNR into one economic entity, then aggregate open AR and credit limits.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track aggregated open exposure of matched entity clusters against the sum of their individually granted credit limits over time.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT STCD1, COUNT(DISTINCT KUNNR) AS Accts, SUM(open_ar) AS TotalExposure, SUM(KLIMK) AS SumLimits FROM KNA1 a JOIN KNKK k ON a.KUNNR=k.KUNNR GROUP BY STCD1 HAVING Accts > 1 AND TotalExposure > single_name_cap -> flag

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Aggregate_Exposure > Single_Name_Cap AND Match_Confidence > 0.9

branchingDAG.hitl: AR Controller approves single-name exposure freeze

hitlGates: AR Controller approves single-name exposure freeze

patterns.name: Ageing Bucket Reset
patterns.mentalModel: The 90-plus bucket keeps emptying at month-end without any cash to explain it. When I trace clearing documents against their re-postings, I see items cleared and immediately re-booked as fresh invoices, resetting the ageing clock so the item looks current again. Collections loses the escalation trigger and my ageing report lies to the auditors. I unwind the churn and restore the true days-past-due.

sap: BSAD (AUGDT, AUGBL, BELNR) - cleared item and clearing doc
BSID (ZFBDT, XBLNR) - re-posted item baseline and reference
CDHDR/CDPOS (TCODE, FNAME, VALUE_NEW) - change docs on baseline date
oracle: AR_PAYMENT_SCHEDULES_ALL (STATUS, GL_DATE) [Fusion] - open vs closed re-ageing

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join cleared AR items to newly posted items sharing the same reference or amount to detect clear-and-reissue cycles that reset the baseline date.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track clearing events immediately followed by same-amount re-postings clustered around period-end close.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT o.KUNNR, o.XBLNR FROM BSAD c JOIN BSID o ON c.XBLNR=o.XBLNR AND c.WRBTR=o.WRBTR WHERE c.AUGDT >= month_end - 3 AND o.ZFBDT > c.AUGDT -> flag AS reage_churn

branchingDAG.hitl: AR Controller approves ageing baseline restore
Audit Committee approves ageing report restatement

hitlGates: AR Controller approves ageing baseline restore
Audit Committee approves ageing report restatement

patterns.name: Month-End Cash Surge
patterns.mentalModel: Cash application goes quiet for three weeks then posts a wall of receipts in the last two days. When I correlate the receipt posting timestamps against actual bank value dates and unapplied balances, the cash arrived earlier but was held and dumped at close to flatter DSO and the month-end cash position. That timing game distorts every metric I report. I re-anchor the postings to bank value date and expose the smoothing.

sap: BSID (AUGDT, CPUDT, DMBTR) - application date vs entry timestamp
FEBEP (VALUT, KWBTR) - bank statement value date and amount
BSEG (UMSKZ, HKONT) - unapplied and on-account holding lines

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join cash application postings to bank statement lines by amount and reference to compare application date against true bank value date.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track daily applied-cash volume and the lag between bank value date and application date across the month.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT a.AUGDT, f.VALUT, DATEDIFF(day, f.VALUT, a.AUGDT) AS ApplyLag FROM BSID a JOIN FEBEP f ON a.DMBTR=f.KWBTR WHERE a.AUGDT >= month_end - 2 AND ApplyLag > 5 -> flag AS held_cash_dump

patterns.name: Intercompany Netting Abuse
patterns.mentalModel: Our external AR looks lean, but I suspect intercompany is doing the heavy lifting. When I separate trading-partner receivables from third-party and check the netting entries against the elimination ledger, I find intercompany balances netted against unrelated external exposure, masking real customer risk and inflating group liquidity. Collections walks away from live third-party debt because it looks netted. I unbundle the netting so external DSO tells the truth.

sap: BSID (VBUND, DMBTR, HKONT) - trading partner and AR line
T001 (RCOMP) - company to consolidation company mapping

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join AR open items to trading-partner codes and elimination-ledger entries to separate intercompany from external receivables and detect cross-netting.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track netting entries that offset intercompany credits against third-party open AR over the close cycle.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT n.VBUND, n.DMBTR FROM BSEG n JOIN BSID x ON n.AUGBL=x.AUGBL WHERE n.VBUND IS NOT NULL AND x.VBUND IS NULL -> flag AS cross_netting_external

patterns.name: Credit Handoff Gap
patterns.mentalModel: Credit approved this account with a risk condition attached, but the collections queue treats it as vanilla. When I join credit-decision change docs to the collection strategy assignment, the risk flag or shortened-terms condition never propagated across the handoff. So we ship on generous terms to an account credit already flagged, and nobody is watching it. I re-apply the risk condition and route it into intensive collection before it ages.

sap: KNKK (KLIMK, DBRTG, CTLPC) - credit limit, rating, risk class
CDHDR/CDPOS (OBJECTID, FNAME, VALUE_NEW) - credit decision change docs
KNB1 (KVERM, MABER) - dunning area and collection assignment

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join credit-decision change documents carrying a risk condition to the customer collection strategy assignment to detect unpropagated risk flags.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track the lag between a credit risk-flag change event and the corresponding collection-strategy update, flagging where it never occurs.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT c.OBJECTID FROM CDPOS c LEFT JOIN IEX_DELINQUENCIES d ON c.OBJECTID=d.CUSTOMER_ID WHERE c.FNAME='DBRTG' AND c.VALUE_NEW IN (high_risk) AND (d.STRATEGY_ID IS NULL OR d.UPDATE_DATE < c.CHANGE_DATE) -> flag

patterns.name: Dispute Deduction Doublecount
patterns.mentalModel: The same customer grievance is bleeding us twice. When I match open dispute cases to deduction residuals on the same invoice and reason code, I find one price discrepancy booked as a formal dispute and again as a short-pay deduction, each reserved separately. Our loss provision and dispute rate are both inflated by the same dollar. I link the two records so we reserve once and recover once.

sap: UDM_DISPUTE (CASE_GUID, OBJKEY, DISPUTED_AMT) - dispute case and invoice
BSID (BELNR, RSTGR, WRBTR) - residual deduction and reason code
BSEG (REBZG, SGTXT) - invoice reference and deduction text
oracle: AR_DISPUTES / OKL (DISPUTE_ID, TRX_ID, AMOUNT) [Fusion] - dispute record

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join open dispute cases to open deduction residuals on the same invoice number and reason code to detect the same issue counted in two ledgers.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track invoices carrying both an active dispute case and an unresolved deduction residual for overlapping amounts.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT d.OBJKEY, d.DISPUTED_AMT, r.WRBTR FROM UDM_DISPUTE d JOIN BSID r ON d.OBJKEY=r.BELNR AND d.RSTGR=r.RSTGR WHERE d.STATUS='OPEN' AND r.WRBTR < 0 -> flag AS double_booked_loss

Audit Committee approves deduction loss restatement

hitlGates: AR Controller approves duplicate reserve release
Audit Committee approves deduction loss restatement

patterns.name: Unapplied Cash Hoarding
patterns.mentalModel: Our reported cash position looks strong, but a growing slice is on-account cash that was never matched to invoices. When I age the unapplied and on-account balances against the open invoices they clearly belong to, cash is deliberately parked unapplied to pad the cash line while the customer still shows as past due in collections. That double-flatters liquidity and ageing at once. I force the application so both ledgers tell the truth.

sap: BSID (UMSKZ, DMBTR, BUDAT) - special GL unapplied and on-account
BSEG (HKONT, ZUONR) - holding account and assignment

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Join aged unapplied and on-account cash balances to the same customer's open invoices to detect matchable cash left deliberately unapplied.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track the ageing of unapplied cash balances against the availability of matchable open invoices over the reporting period.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT u.KUNNR, u.DMBTR, p.AMOUNT_DUE_REMAINING FROM BSID u JOIN AR_PAYMENT_SCHEDULES_ALL p ON u.KUNNR=p.CUSTOMER_ID WHERE u.UMSKZ IN (unapplied_gl) AND DATEDIFF(day,u.BUDAT,today) > 15 AND p.AMOUNT_DUE_REMAINING > 0 -> flag

branchingDAG.hitl: AR Controller approves forced cash application

hitlGates: AR Controller approves forced cash application

patterns.name: Threshold Splitting WriteOffs
patterns.mentalModel: Every write-off lands one dollar under someone's approval ceiling, and that is no accident. When I histogram write-off and deduction amounts against the delegation-of-authority thresholds and group them by customer and clerk, I see large balances sliced into sub-threshold pieces to dodge controller sign-off. The bad-debt leaks through in a thousand small cuts across disputes and deductions. I aggregate the slices back to their true size and pull them into review.

sap: BSEG (DMBTR, HKONT, BSCHL) - write-off amount and posting key
BKPF (USNAM, TCODE, BUDAT) - user and transaction for clustering
T042Z / auth tables (approval thresholds) - delegation-of-authority limits
oracle: AR_ADJUSTMENTS_ALL (AMOUNT, TYPE, CREATED_BY) [Fusion] - adjustment and author

patterns.layer1_logicalMapping: Group write-off and deduction postings by customer and clerk and compare each amount and its cumulative to the delegation-of-authority thresholds.
patterns.layer2_eventSeries: Track clustering of write-off amounts just below approval ceilings and their per-customer cumulative over a rolling window.
patterns.conceptualSQL: SELECT USNAM, KUNNR, COUNT(*) AS Slices, SUM(DMBTR) AS TrueTotal FROM BSEG b JOIN BKPF h ON b.BELNR=h.BELNR WHERE DMBTR BETWEEN 0.9*threshold AND threshold GROUP BY USNAM, KUNNR HAVING Slices > 2 AND TrueTotal > threshold -> flag
